compiled_state.title: File impact as first-class ticket field with Jira bridge surfacing
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
## Context

Sprint orchestrators today cannot reliably determine which files a task will touch before dispatching agents — file impact data is stored as free-text markdown embedded in ticket comments or descriptions, parsed ad-hoc by 4 scripts (sprint-next-batch.sh, sprint-drift-check.sh, sprint-review-scope-check.sh, enrich-file-impact.sh). Each script reimplements its own markdown parser, producing inconsistent extraction results that cause agent conflicts and missed drift detection. This epic gives orchestrators a structured, queryable file impact field so conflict detection and drift checks operate on authoritative data rather than best-effort text extraction. When the ticket system is eventually extracted into an MCP server, file impact will be a structured, queryable field — not a convention buried in free text.

## Success Criteria

- A FILE_IMPACT event type is supported by the ticket system: ticket-lib.sh write_commit_event() accepts FILE_IMPACT in its event type enum, and ticket-reducer.py compiles FILE_IMPACT events into a structured file_impact field in the ticket's compiled state — containing an array of {path, reason} objects with latest-wins semantics
- ticket set-file-impact <id> <json> stores structured file impact data as a FILE_IMPACT event; ticket get-file-impact <id> returns the structured array; ticket show <id> includes the file_impact field in its output
- The Jira outbound bridge surfaces file impact in Jira using two mechanisms: (a) a Jira issue property (key: dso.file_impact, set via direct REST API PUT to /rest/api/3/issue/{key}/properties/dso.file_impact using AcliClient._direct_rest_put() — a new method justified by spike confirming ACLI has no properties subcommand) for machine-readable storage, and (b) a single formatted comment (deduped by UUID marker via the existing comment dedup mechanism and .jira-comment-map) for human visibility. The inbound bridge ignores the dso.file_impact property (properties live outside fields.*) and deduplicates the outbound comment via the existing dedup map. Bridge auth: if API token, no scope change required; if OAuth 2.0, confirm write:jira-work scope is present
- bridge-outbound.py process_outbound() handles FILE_IMPACT events explicitly — they do not fall through silently. When issue property PUT fails, a BRIDGE_ALERT event is written with reason FILE_IMPACT_SYNC_FAILED; when comment add fails, a distinct BRIDGE_ALERT event is written with reason FILE_IMPACT_COMMENT_SYNC_FAILED; no transactional rollback across the two channels
- update-artifacts.sh is extended with a data migration phase: the first registered migration scans all tickets for ## File Impact patterns in COMMENT event bodies and description text, extracts file paths, and writes corresponding FILE_IMPACT events. Success: all tickets with parseable patterns have a FILE_IMPACT event; unparseable tickets logged to stderr with ticket ID and skipped; re-running on migrated tickets is a no-op (stamp prevents re-run, keyed by migration ID file-impact-v1). Stamp records {version, migrated_at, tickets_processed, tickets_skipped}. Test suite includes well-formed fixture, malformed fixture, and idempotency case
- enrich-file-impact.sh is refactored: LLM generation (Haiku API call) stays in the workflow layer; storage changes from ticket comment to ticket set-file-impact. sprint-drift-check.sh and sprint-review-scope-check.sh are also refactored to use ticket get-file-impact instead of ad-hoc markdown parsing — all four ad-hoc parsers migrate to the structured API. Markdown parsers preserved as SECONDARY fallback for tickets pre-migration

## Dependencies

- Epic A1 (2c28-e5b9 — Ticket System Core Query Layer): partial — sprint-next-batch.sh integration via ticket next-batch needs A1's subcommand. All other SCs in this epic are unblocked. Stories covering sprint-next-batch.sh integration should be sequenced after A1 delivers ticket next-batch.

## Approach

FILE_IMPACT event type follows the existing event pattern (JSON file in ticket directory, atomic commit via flock). Jira bridge uses a new _direct_rest_put() method on AcliClient — AcliClient already stores credentials on self with a comment noting they're available for direct REST calls; _direct_rest_put() contains the ACLI-only pattern break cleanly with unit tests mocking the PUT and asserting credential header injection. Permissions: Browse projects + Edit issues (same auth bridge already has with API tokens).

## Research Findings

- Jira REST API: `PUT /rest/api/3/issue/{key}/properties/{propertyKey}` is the canonical mechanism for structured machine-readable metadata that should NOT appear in fields.* (issue properties live outside the field schema and are ignored by the inbound bridge correctly)
- ACLI limitation confirmed via spike: ACLI has no `properties` subcommand — justifies the _direct_rest_put() pattern break; method reuses same credential mechanism (_build_env) as existing ACLI subprocess calls for credential continuity
- Permissions: `write:jira-work` scope (OAuth 2.0) or standard API token with Browse projects + Edit issues — same scope existing bridge already has; no new scope request required
- UUID-marker dedup: existing .jira-comment-map mechanism handles comment deduplication; new FILE_IMPACT comment uses a new UUID marker category
- Migration pattern: update-artifacts.sh supports versioned migrations via stamp files; file-impact-v1 stamp records {version, migrated_at, tickets_processed, tickets_skipped} for idempotency and audit
- Reducer semantics: latest-wins is the chosen pattern for current-state fields (file impact describes what a ticket's current scope is, not its history)

## Scenario Analysis

- Jira property PUT succeeds but comment add fails (medium, integration): distinct BRIDGE_ALERT reason FILE_IMPACT_COMMENT_SYNC_FAILED for comment-channel failure; no transactional rollback across channels — property succeeds, comment retries on next sync
- Inbound bridge re-reads dso.file_impact property and tries to write back (high, sync loop): inbound ignores the property explicitly; FILE_IMPACT events originate from DSO only — Jira is a one-way surface
- Migration re-runs and duplicates events (high, correctness): stamp file with file-impact-v1 key prevents re-run; idempotency test case in migration test suite
- Migration parses malformed markdown and creates corrupt events (medium, correctness): unparseable entries logged to stderr with ticket ID and skipped; original markdown preserved for manual triage
- Latest-wins reducer semantics clobber earlier FILE_IMPACT events (medium, design): explicit latest-wins is the chosen semantic — documented in SC1 and reducer tests; intentional because file impact is current-state, not historical
- _direct_rest_put() bypasses ACLI's retry/rate-limit handling (medium, reliability): method inherits credential mechanism but not retry logic — basic retry with exponential backoff added in the method; documented limitation
- bridge-outbound FILE_IMPACT branch silently falls through when event handler missing (high, integration): explicit enum check in dispatcher; unknown event types emit BRIDGE_ALERT UNKNOWN_EVENT_TYPE rather than silent drop

### Planning Intelligence Log

- **Web research (Step 2.6)**: triggered
  - Bright-line conditions that fired: external integration (Jira REST API `/rest/api/3/issue/{key}/properties/{propertyKey}` endpoint, AcliClient pattern break), novel architectural pattern (FILE_IMPACT event type + latest-wins reducer semantics for structured current-state fields)
- **Scenario analysis (Step 2.75)**: triggered
  - Scenarios surviving blue team filter: 7
- **Practitioner-requested additional cycles**: none
- **Follow-on scrutiny (Step 0)**: not triggered
- **Feasibility resolution (Step 2.5)**: triggered — ACLI properties-subcommand spike confirmed absence, justifying _direct_rest_put() pattern break
- **LLM-instruction signal (Step 5)**: not triggered — this epic is data schema + bridge integration; no LLM-facing instruction changes
- **Scale context (Step 0)**: small scale (default) — per-ticket file-impact storage; write volume bounded by ticket edit frequency; not volume-sensitive
